title: World History
description: Question batch for the Ancient Civilizations domain.

questions.question: Which river supported ancient Egyptian civilization?
questions.explanation: Egypt grew along the Nile River.

questions.question: Which writing system is linked to ancient Egypt?
questions.explanation: Hieroglyphs were used in ancient Egypt.

questions.question: Which civilization built ziggurats in Mesopotamia?
questions.explanation: Sumerian cities are known for ziggurats.

questions.question: What was the main writing system of Mesopotamia?
questions.explanation: Mesopotamians wrote on clay tablets in cuneiform.

questions.question: Which ruler is tied to an early Babylonian law code?
questions.explanation: Hammurabi is known for his law code.

questions.question: Which civilization developed in the Indus Valley?
questions.explanation: The Harappan civilization flourished in the Indus Valley.

questions.question: Which civilization built Machu Picchu?
questions.explanation: Machu Picchu was built by the Inca.

questions.question: Which people are known for city-states like Athens and Sparta?
questions.explanation: Ancient Greece was made up of city-states.

questions.question: Which empire centered on Rome?
questions.explanation: Rome was the center of the Roman Empire.

questions.question: Which sea was central to Phoenician trade?
questions.explanation: Phoenician merchants sailed the Mediterranean.

questions.question: Which Chinese dynasty is linked to the first emperor?
questions.explanation: Qin Shi Huang founded the Qin dynasty empire.

questions.question: Which philosophy is associated with Confucius?
questions.explanation: Confucius taught ideas later called Confucianism.

questions.question: What was a key Roman engineering achievement?
questions.explanation: Romans built aqueducts to move water.

questions.question: Which civilization is known for democracy in Athens?
questions.explanation: Athens is a famous example of early democracy.

questions.question: Which empire used roads like the Royal Road?
questions.explanation: The Persian Empire used the Royal Road for control and trade.

questions.question: Which ancient wonder stood at Giza?
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid is at Giza in Egypt.

questions.question: Which city is associated with the Parthenon?
questions.explanation: The Parthenon stands on the Acropolis in Athens.

questions.question: Which empire fought Carthage in the Punic Wars?
questions.explanation: Rome and Carthage fought the Punic Wars.

questions.question: Which civilization is linked to the city of Teotihuacan?
questions.explanation: Teotihuacan was a major Mesoamerican city.

questions.question: Which crop was central to Maya and Aztec diets?
questions.explanation: Maize was a staple crop in Mesoamerica.

questions.question: What made the Nile vital to Egypt?
questions.explanation: Regular floods enriched farmland along the Nile.

questions.question: Why was cuneiform important?
questions.explanation: Writing helped manage trade, laws, and government.

questions.question: Which city was famous for hanging gardens in tradition?
questions.explanation: Ancient tradition places the Hanging Gardens in Babylon.

questions.question: Which civilization spread an alphabet influencing later scripts?
questions.explanation: The Phoenician alphabet influenced Greek and Latin scripts.

questions.question: Which Roman period began with Augustus?
questions.explanation: Augustus became the first Roman emperor.

questions.question: Which Indian emperor promoted Buddhism after Kalinga?
questions.explanation: Ashoka later promoted Buddhist teachings.

questions.question: What was a major Silk Road effect?
questions.explanation: The Silk Road spread trade, culture, and technology.

questions.question: Which civilization is known for terracotta warriors?
questions.explanation: The Terracotta Army guarded Qin Shi Huang's tomb.

questions.question: Why were Roman roads important?
answers: They improved movement of armies and trade
questions.explanation: Roads helped Rome connect and control its empire.

questions.question: Which city was destroyed by Vesuvius in 79 CE?
questions.explanation: Pompeii was buried by the eruption of Vesuvius.
